proposed_hypotheses.text: Univariate: feature_006 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006 = 0.
Univariate: feature_013 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_013 = 0.
Univariate: feature_001 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_001 = 0.
Univariate: feature_028 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_028 = 0.
Univariate: feature_029 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_029 = 0.
Univariate: feature_018 = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_018 = 0.
Univariate: feature_023 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_023 = 0.
Univariate: feature_016 = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_016 = 0.
Continuous: feature_012 (range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
Continuous: feature_024 is positively correlated with pfs_months.
Continuous: feature_004 is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Continuous: feature_033 is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
Ordinal: feature_005 (0/1/2) is monotonically inversely associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Slope = +0.176 months per unit, p ~ 0; Spearman rho = +0.726. Dominant predictor: PFS rises from 2.28 (Q1: 30-56.5) to 7.21 (Q5: 73.6-90).
Slope = +0.499 mo per unit, p=9e-112; Spearman rho = +0.102.
Slope = -0.0747 mo per unit, p=4e-148; Spearman rho = -0.116.
Slope = -0.0188 mo per unit, p=3e-93; Spearman rho = -0.083 (univariate effect partly conditional, see later).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Multivariable OLS of pfs_months on all 37 features: feature_012, feature_005, feature_006, feature_013, feature_001, feature_004, feature_028, feature_024, feature_033, feature_029, and feature_018 are independently significant predictors after mutual adjustment.

Most other features (feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_009, feature_026, feature_015, feature_022, feature_008, feature_032, feature_020, feature_036, feature_021, feature_003, feature_025, feature_027, feature_034, feature_014, feature_037, feature_017, feature_035, feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_016, feature_023, feature_031) carry no independent prognostic signal once the leading predictors are adjusted for.

analyses.result_summary: After mutual adjustment, p>0.05 for feature_011, _030, _019, _009 (p=0.04, borderline), _026, _015, _022, _008, _032, _020, _036, _021, _003, _025, _027, _034, _014, _037, _017, _035, _002, _007, _010, _016, _023. Their univariate signals (where present) are absorbed by correlated leading predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 18 binary features, feature_029 and feature_023 are highly correlated (likely a positive biomarker present in most patients with feature_029).
feature_018 and feature_016 are mutually exclusive in their (1) cells: no patient has both feature_018=1 and feature_016=1.
feature_005 (the ordinal stage-like variable) is essentially independent of the 18 binary features (similar prevalences across f005=0/1/2).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on each binary pair, phi coefficient
analyses.result_summary: Strongest association is feature_029 x feature_023 (phi=0.669, p~0). Joint dist: among feature_029=1 patients, 85% are also feature_023=1 (29674/34886); among feature_029=0, only 15% are feature_023=1.
feature_018=1 cell has 9060 patients, ALL with feature_016=0. feature_016=1 only occurs when feature_018=0 (phi=0.361). They appear to be mutually exclusive markers.
All 18 binary features have prevalences within +/-0.01 across the three feature_005 strata. feature_005 is independent of binary covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a substantial binary x binary interaction in pfs_months between feature_029 and feature_013: feature_013's positive effect is much larger when feature_029 = 1.
feature_018 negatively interacts with feature_013: feature_018 = 1 abolishes the apparent benefit of feature_013.
feature_028 negatively interacts with feature_013: feature_028 = 1 abolishes the apparent benefit of feature_013.
feature_023 also positively interacts with feature_013, but this is confounded by its strong correlation with feature_029.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction f013:f029 = +1.594 mo, p=9.3e-229. Stratified: f013 effect = -0.017 mo when f029=0 (p=0.63); f013 effect = +1.577 mo when f029=1 (p~0).
Interaction = -1.364 mo, p=2.2e-118. Stratified within f029=1: f013 effect = +1.92 when f018=0 vs +0.02 when f018=1.
Interaction = -1.623 mo, p=4.0e-255 (largest binary x binary interaction). Stratified within f029=1: f013 effect when f028=0 is much larger than when f028=1.
Interaction = +1.093 mo, p=2.6e-117. Stratified: f013 effect = +0.40 when f023=0 vs +1.49 when f023=1, but feature_023 is largely a marker for feature_029 (phi=0.669).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stratifying simultaneously by feature_029 and feature_018: feature_013 confers a benefit only in (feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0).

analyses.code: 4-way stratified t-test of f013 effect over f029 x f018

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding feature_028 as a third stratifier: feature_013's benefit is restricted to (feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0). The diff is roughly +2.9 months in this 8-cell partition's responder cell.

analyses.code: 8-way stratified table over f029 x f018 x f028
analyses.result_summary: f013 effect across 8 cells: only (f29=1,f18=0,f28=0) shows benefit: PFS f013=0 = 4.300, f013=1 = 7.209, diff=+2.908, p~0; n=18681. All other 7 cells have |diff|<0.13, p>0.2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within (f029=1 AND f018=0 AND f028=0), feature_033 is a continuous effect modifier of feature_013: PFS benefit is large when feature_033 is low and absent when feature_033 is high.

analyses.code: deciles of f033 within the 3-way responder cell, stratified t-tests
analyses.result_summary: f033 deciles in responder cell: D1-D6 (f033 ≤ 14.6) all show f013 benefit ≈ +4.0 to +5.0 mo (each p<1e-150). D7-D10 (f033 ≥ 14.7): f013 effects are ≈ 0 (all |diff|<0.25, p>0.02).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a clear threshold near feature_033 = 14: below this, feature_013 confers a roughly constant +5 month PFS benefit; above this the benefit collapses to near zero.

analyses.code: sliding cutoff search; piecewise OLS within responder cell
analyses.result_summary: Effect within (f29=1,f18=0,f28=0): for f033≤14, diff=+4.943 (n=10828, p~0); for f033>14, diff=+0.024 (n=7853, p=0.62). Bin-by-bin: f33<14 yields ≈+5; first attenuation at [14,15) (+0.61), zero by [16,18). Threshold ≈ 14 is sharp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 does NOT add additional effect modification beyond feature_029 within the responder cell (it is a redundant proxy).
feature_016 does NOT independently modify the feature_013 effect once the (f029,f018,f028,f033) profile is fixed.

analyses.result_summary: f023=0: diff=+4.330 (n=1711); f023=1: diff=+4.705 (n=9864). Difference between strata small (~0.4 mo) given confidence. f023 mainly tags f029 status.

analyses.code: f013 effect within responder by f016
analyses.result_summary: In full data, the f013 x f016 interaction is +0.42 (p=9e-19), but this is mediated through f018 (f016=1 implies f018=0). Within responder cell stratified by f016, the conditional difference is small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 is a uniform prognostic marker: it lowers pfs_months by ~1 mo regardless of feature_029, feature_018, feature_028, or feature_005, with no interaction.
feature_006 is a uniform prognostic marker: lowers pfs_months by ~1.5 mo across feature_005 strata, with at most a small modifier by feature_012.

analyses.code: f001 effect across f29 x f18 strata; across f005
analyses.result_summary: f001 diff in 4 (f29,f18) cells: -1.079, -1.071, -1.064, -0.953 (all p<1e-25). Across f005 (0/1/2): -1.056, -1.078, -0.873. Highly consistent. No significant interaction with any other binary (all p>0.05). It behaves as an independent uniform negative prognostic.

analyses.code: f006 effect across f005 strata; f006 x continuous
analyses.result_summary: f006 diff: -1.599 (f005=0), -1.506 (f005=1), -1.482 (f005=2). Only continuous interaction is f006 x f012 (-0.011 per unit, p=3e-12; small magnitude, ~ -0.66 mo extra at f012=90 vs 30). Otherwise homogeneous.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028's apparent main effect (-0.56 mo) is entirely driven by its conditional effect within (feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_013=1): in that cell f028=1 reverses the +5 month treatment benefit; in all other cells it has zero effect.
feature_028 should therefore be classified as a NEGATIVE PREDICTIVE BIOMARKER for feature_013 rather than as a prognostic factor or an independent treatment.

analyses.code: f028 effect across all 8 cells of f029 x f018 x f013
analyses.result_summary: f028 effect: zero (p>0.2) in 7 of 8 cells. The single cell with effect: (f29=1,f18=0,f13=1): f028 diff = -2.852 mo (p~0, n_t=3400). Compare (f29=1,f18=0,f13=0): f028 diff = +0.034 (p=0.29).

analyses.code: interpretation: f028 only matters when f013 is given to f029=1,f18=0 patients
analyses.result_summary: This pattern is the signature of a treatment-resistance biomarker: f028 is silent in untreated patients but blocks response to f013 in the otherwise-responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Three-way interaction f013 x f028 x f029 is highly significant: feature_028's resistance effect requires feature_029 = 1 (because feature_013 has no effect when feature_029=0).

Three-way interaction f013 x f028 x f018 is highly significant in the OPPOSITE direction (positive coefficient): when feature_018=1, feature_028's resistance effect disappears (because the treatment is already inactive).

analyses.result_summary: 3-way coefficient = -2.428 mo, p=1.6e-128. Confirms feature_028 reverses feature_013's benefit specifically in feature_029=1 patients.

3-way coefficient = +2.054 mo, p=5.9e-65. Sign indicates that within f018=1 (no benefit baseline), f028 cannot subtract any benefit, so the apparent f013:f028 interaction is attenuated -- consistent with the responder cell being defined by f029=1 AND f018=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint OLS with f013 main effect plus f013:f029, f013:f018, f013:f028, f013:f033 captures all interaction structure and increases R^2 from 0.79 to ~0.86.

analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.858. Coefficients: f013 (baseline) = +1.665; f13:f29 = +1.530; f13:f18 = -1.324; f13:f28 = -1.643; f13:f33 = -0.0527/unit. All p~0. Main effects of f029, f018, f028, f023 essentially zero after controlling for interactions: f029=-0.010 (p=0.49), f018=+0.004 (p=0.74), f028=-0.003 (p=0.76), f023=+0.013 (p=0.29). i.e. these features are PURELY predictive (treatment-effect modifiers), not prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is randomly distributed (~35% prevalence) across all biomarker strata: f029, f018, f028, f023. This is consistent with feature_013 being an experimentally assigned treatment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013 prevalence: by f029 (0.346, 0.350); by f018 (0.347, 0.355); by f028 (0.349, 0.347); by f023 (0.347, 0.349); overall 0.349. Differences <1%. No biomarker-driven assignment bias.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder cell (f029=1, f018=0, f028=0), the relationship between feature_013 effect and feature_033 is approximately: ~+5 month constant benefit when f033 ≤ ~14, near-zero benefit above that threshold (i.e. a step function rather than a smooth linear decay).

analyses.code: piecewise OLS in responder cell with break at f033=14
analyses.result_summary: In responder cell: linear OLS gives f013 effect = 5.008 - 0.135*f033 (R^2 fit suggests some curvature). Bin-by-bin: f013 diff is +4.9 to +5.1 across all bins of f033<14, then drops to +0.6 in [14,15), and ~0 thereafter. Empirical pattern is best described as step at f033≈14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final responder definition: feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033 ≤ 14. In this subgroup feature_013 increases pfs_months by ~+4.94 months. Outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup size: 10828 (21.7% of cohort). Inside: f013=0 PFS=4.285 vs f013=1 PFS=9.228, diff=+4.943 mo, p~0. Outside (n=39172): f013=0 PFS=4.317 vs f013=1 PFS=4.324, diff=+0.007 mo, p=0.75.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the responder × treatment interaction is added to the prognostic-only OLS, the model explains nearly all variance (R^2 > 0.99): the data-generating mechanism appears to be additive prognostic main effects + a single conditional treatment effect of ~+5 months in the defined responder subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.9947. Significant: f012 (+0.177, t=2157), f005 (-1.170, t=-973), f006 (-1.557, t=-875), f001 (-0.971, t=-360), f004 (-0.078, t=-368), f024 (+0.488, t=300), f031 (-5e-4, t=-51), responder×f013 (+4.977, t=1203). Non-significant: f013 alone, f029, f018, f028, f023, f033, f036, responder. The conditional treatment-effect interaction captures essentially all remaining variance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 18 binary features, ONLY feature_013 shows a heterogeneous treatment-like effect with a defined responder cell. The other binaries with strong main effects (feature_006, feature_001) act as uniform prognostic markers; feature_029, feature_018, feature_028, feature_023 act as predictive biomarkers for feature_013; feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_009, feature_026, feature_015, feature_022, feature_008, feature_032, feature_020, feature_016 carry no detectable signal.

analyses.code: count of significant pairwise interactions per binary
analyses.result_summary: Counts of binary×binary interactions with p<1e-20: feature_028 (5), feature_013 (5), feature_029 (4), feature_018 (3), feature_023 (2). All others 0. With p<0.001: feature_013, feature_028 (6), feature_029, feature_018 (5), feature_023 (4), feature_016 (3). Confirms a single interaction cluster centered on feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous-feature × treatment screen: among continuous features only feature_033 strongly modifies feature_013 (interaction = -0.0524 per unit, p=1e-174). feature_012 weakly modifies feature_006 (interaction -0.011, p=3e-12). All other binary × continuous interactions are at most weakly significant.

analyses.code: OLS y ~ binary + cont + binary:cont for top 8 binaries x 18 continuous
analyses.result_summary: Top binary×continuous interactions on pfs_months: f013 x f033 (-0.0524, p=1e-174); f028 x f033 (+0.0298, p=6e-56); f029 x f033 (-0.0254, p=1e-37); f018 x f033 (+0.0211, p=1e-18); f023 x f033 (-0.0158, p=1e-16); f006 x f012 (-0.0112, p=3e-12); f001 x f012 (-0.0076, p=3e-3). The f033 interactions all align with feature_033 being the threshold modifier within the responder cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (ordinal stage-like variable) does NOT modify feature_013's effect (no f013 × f005 interaction).
feature_005 also does not modify feature_006 or feature_001 effects.

analyses.result_summary: Best p-value: f006 x f005 = +0.067, p=0.040 (borderline; very small magnitude). All other binary x f005 interactions p>0.05. f013 x f005 interaction is small and non-significant. f005 contributes only as an additive prognostic factor.
f001 x f005 effect ≈ uniform across levels (-1.05 vs -1.08 vs -0.87). No interaction with f005.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028, feature_018, feature_023, feature_033 are PURELY PREDICTIVE biomarkers for feature_013 -- they have negligible prognostic effect of their own once the responder×treatment term is included.
feature_029 is dominantly predictive (gating biomarker) but may carry a tiny additive prognostic signal absorbed by the responder term.

analyses.result_summary: In the model that includes responder×f013, all four candidate predictive markers have non-significant main effects: f029 p=0.68, f018 p=0.10, f028 p=0.32, f023 p=0.69; their univariate prognostic appearances arose entirely from confounding with the treatment-responder structure.
f029 main effect after responder×f013 = 0.001 (p=0.68). No detectable residual prognostic signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tree-style subgroup discovery (manual): split sequentially on f029, f018, f028, then continuous f033. The terminal leaf (f29=1, f18=0, f28=0, f33<=14, f013=1) has the highest predicted pfs_months in the cohort (~9.2 months).
No other terminal leaf shows a comparable conditional treatment effect for any other binary; this confirms feature_013 is the unique treatment in the dataset.

analyses.code: manual recursive partitioning over (f029,f018,f028,f033) within f013=0 vs f013=1
analyses.result_summary: Means in 16 cells of f013×(f29,f18,f28,f33≤14): only the cell (f013=1, f29=1, f18=0, f28=0, f33≤14) has mean 9.228 (n=3853). Counterfactual cell (f013=0 with same biomarkers) has mean 4.285 (n=6975). All other 14 cells fall in 3.5-5.0 month range.

analyses.code: check f028 effect in absence of f013, f018 effect in absence of f013
analyses.result_summary: In f013=0 cohort (n=32572) effects of f028, f018, f029, f023, f033 are all near zero. They only express conditional effects through their interaction with f013. Hence f013 is the unique active treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sensitivity analysis: relaxing the responder cell by removing the f028=0 condition (keeping only f29=1, f18=0, f33<=14) lowers the average treatment effect, because the f028=1 patients within the new cell have zero benefit. Removing f018=0 has the same effect.

analyses.code: f013 effect in (f29=1, f18=0, f33<=14) ignoring f028; etc.
analyses.result_summary: f29=1,f18=0,f33<=14 (n=14979): f013 diff = +3.36 (smaller because the 4151 f028=1 patients contribute zero). f29=1,f33<=14 (n=18308): f013 diff = +2.69 (further dilution by f018=1 patients). f33<=14 (n=27116): f013 diff = +2.10. Each predicate (f29=1, f18=0, f28=0, f33<=14) strictly improves enrichment, so all four are required for the cleanest definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: in the breast cancer cohort, the binary therapy variable feature_013 INCREASES pfs_months by approximately +4.94 months in patients meeting ALL of: feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_033 <= 14. Outside this subgroup the treatment effect is null. feature_023 is a redundant proxy for feature_029 and is not required in the predicate. The four predicates are jointly required: dropping any one substantially dilutes the estimated treatment effect.

Independent additive prognostic factors that affect pfs_months but do NOT modify treatment effect: feature_012 (positive, ~+0.18 mo per unit), feature_005 (negative, ~-1.17 mo per ordinal level), feature_006 (negative, ~-1.55 mo), feature_001 (negative, ~-0.97 mo), feature_004 (negative, ~-0.077 mo per unit), feature_024 (positive, ~+0.49 mo per unit), feature_031 (negative, ~-5e-4 mo per unit). These are uniform across treatment status and biomarker subgroups.

analyses.code: final stratified verification (see iteration 17) and OLS (iteration 18)
analyses.result_summary: In subgroup defined by (f029=1, f018=0, f028=0, f033<=14): n=10828, f013=0 PFS=4.285 (n=6975), f013=1 PFS=9.228 (n=3853), Δ=+4.943 months, p~0. Outside (n=39172): Δ=+0.007 months, p=0.75. The full-feature OLS with this responder×f013 interaction reaches R^2=0.9947, with the responder×f013 coefficient at +4.977 mo (t=1203, p~0).

All listed prognostic features remain highly significant after including responder×f013: f012 (+0.177, p~0), f005 (-1.170, p~0), f006 (-1.557, p~0), f001 (-0.971, p~0), f004 (-0.078, p~0), f024 (+0.488, p~0), f031 (-5e-4, p~0), f033 (-1e-4, p=0.04 marginal). f036 not significant (p=0.33). All non-listed binaries already removed at iteration 3 are still null. Total cohort R^2 = 0.9947.
